symptoms.symptom_name: Exertional dyspnoea and fatigue, progressive over months with acute worsening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced forward output and pulmonary congestion cause exertional breathlessness and fatigue.
Exertional dyspnoea is shared by airways disease; not discriminating on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthopnoea relieved by sitting up (three pillows)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Orthopnoea reflects redistribution of pulmonary blood volume on lying flat with raised left-heart filling pressures.
Posture-dependent breathlessness relieved by sitting is far more typical of cardiac congestion than airways disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea with nocturnal dry cough

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: PND is a relatively specific symptom of left-heart failure with nocturnal pulmonary congestion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral dependent ankle oedema by end of day

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dependent oedema reflects fluid overload, though it is non-specific in isolation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dyspnoea at rest with self-reported tachypnoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Breathlessness at rest indicates significant congestion and limited reserve — a marker of acuity driving escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of chest pain and palpitations

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduces but does not exclude ACS — silent ischaemia can precipitate decompensation, so ECG/troponin are still required.

symptoms.symptom_name: No prior cardiac/respiratory diagnosis and not on any regular medication

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An untreated, previously undiagnosed structural lesion can present as first decompensation; absence of cardiac medication does not lower risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Loud apical pansystolic (holosystolic) murmur radiating to the axilla

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pansystolic murmur loudest at the apex radiating to the axilla is the classic auscultatory signature of mitral regurgitation — sealed clinician finding.
Aortic stenosis gives an ejection systolic murmur at the base radiating to the carotids, not this pattern.
Reflects a dilated, volume-loaded left ventricle — sealed clinician palpation finding.
Mitral stenosis produces a tapping, non-displaced apex rather than a hyperdynamic displaced one.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft first heart sound (S1) and third heart sound (S3) gallop

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Soft S1 fits incompetent mitral valve closure; an S3 marks rapid ventricular filling in a volume-overloaded, failing ventricle — sealed clinician findings.
Bibasal crackles indicate early pulmonary oedema from raised left-heart pressures — sealed clinician finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Resting hypoxia SpO2 94% with tachypnoea (RR 20) — clinician-measured

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild resting hypoxia and tachypnoea reflect pulmonary congestion; measured by the in-person clinician, not available via chat unless a home oximeter is offered.

symptoms.symptom_name: Echocardiogram showing a backward (regurgitant) systolic jet across the mitral valve

investigation_details.expected_result: Regurgitant mitral jet with dilated volume-loaded left ventricle; quantifies severity and LV function
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity and LV function undefined; surgical/structural decision cannot be made.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct visualisation of the regurgitant jet confirms and grades mitral regurgitation — the reference standard, sealed.

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated, consistent with ventricular wall stress from congestion
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Loses an objective marker supporting a cardiac cause of the dyspnoea.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A raised natriuretic peptide supports heart failure and argues against a purely respiratory cause — hospital/lab investigation.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Orthopnoea, PND, dependent oedema and rest dyspnoea together strongly indicate left-heart decompensation and drive urgent escalation.

Apical pansystolic murmur radiating to axilla, displaced hyperdynamic apex, soft S1 and an echocardiographic regurgitant jet form the diagnostic tetrad — all requiring in-person examination or investigation, sealed from the AI Doctor.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screen for ischaemia/arrhythmia precipitating decompensation and for LV strain; requires in-person facilities.
Confirm pulmonary congestion/oedema and cardiomegaly; exclude pneumonia.
Objective marker supporting a cardiac cause of the breathlessness.
Defines the valve lesion severity and LV function — the reference test for mitral regurgitation.
Baseline renal function and electrolytes before and during diuretic therapy.
Exclude anaemia as an aggravating contributor to dyspnoea and fatigue.
Exclude acute coronary syndrome as a precipitant despite absence of chest pain.
The clinical picture is congestive heart failure, not pulmonary embolism; routine D-dimer/CTPA is not indicated and would expose the patient to unnecessary contrast and radiation.
